data.title: Verses AI Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Verses AI Inc., a leading innovator in agentic software platforms, has released its Q1 2026 10-Q report, providing a comprehensive overview of its financial and operational performance. The report highlights the company's efforts in launching new products, expanding its market reach, and navigating financial challenges.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0.82 million, reflecting an increase from the previous year, driven by the launch of the Genius platform.
  • Gross Profit: $0.13 million, indicating a positive margin despite high operating expenses.
  • Operating Income (Expense): $(24.90) million, showing a significant operating loss due to high selling, general, and administrative expenses.
  • Net Loss: $(24.98) million, primarily due to substantial operating expenses and other expenses.
  • Loss Per Class A Subordinate Voting Shares - Basic and Diluted: $(2.50), reflecting the impact of the net loss on shareholders.

Business Highlights

  • New Product Launches: In April 2025, Verses AI Inc. launched its flagship product, Genius, an innovative agentic software platform. Genius uses proprietary technology based on Active Inference to support and improve domain-specific decision-making by providing insights in volatile, uncertain, complex, or ambiguous data environments.
  • Revenue Segments: The company recognized revenue from contracts with customers, including Proof of Concept contracts and software implementation services, as well as Software as a Service (SaaS) offerings. Revenue was recognized both at a point in time and over the duration of contracts.
  • Operational Strategy: Verses AI Inc. has implemented a 'Lighthouse' strategy, focusing on licensing an enterprise version of Genius to financial institutions and asset managers managing over $100 million in assets. This strategy targets high-value entities such as pension funds, sovereign funds, insurance companies, and hedge funds.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to expand its target sectors beyond financial institutions to include robotics, logistics, and infrastructure management. This expansion is based on real-world results and feedback from initial target clients, which will help further develop and improve the Genius platform.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates primarily in the United States, with no additional operations or sources of revenue outside the country. The Chief Operating Decision Maker evaluates performance based on the company's net income as reported in the Statements of Operations.
  • Management Changes: On February 8, 2026, Gabriel Rene resigned as Chief Executive Officer and director. David Scott, a current board member, was appointed as interim CEO while a search for a permanent replacement is conducted. Dan Mapes, President Emeritus and Global Brand Ambassador, also resigned.
  • Business Model: Verses AI Inc. offers a range of pricing packages for Genius, including flat fee, profit share, and variable pricing options based on complexity and usage criteria such as the number of users, transactions, and input parameters.

SEC Filing: Verses AI Inc. [ VRSSF ] - 10-Q - Feb. 20, 2026

data.title: Blue Owl Stock Rises After Saba Capital And Cox Capital’s Tender Offers For BDC Shares
data.description:
Key points:
  • Both the firms sent the notice to Blue Owl Capital Corporation II on February 17, 2026 of their intention to commence a tender offer to purchase a portion of outstanding shares of OBDC II in cash.
  • The tender offers would provide a liquidity solution to retail investors in the wake of a significant industry-wide increase in BDC redemption requests.
  • Blue Owl said on Thursday that it will permanently stop allowing redemptions from Blue Owl Capital Corp. II, a semi-liquid private credit fund marketed to U.S. retail investors.

Blue Owl Capital got a reprieve on Friday after Saba Capital and Cox Capital Partners announced cash tender offers for shares in business development companies (BDC) that are owned by Blue Owl. 

The two companies subsequently notified Blue Owl Technology Income Corp and Blue Owl Credit Income Corp of their intention to commence similar tender offers.

At the time of writing OWL stock was up 2.2% in extended hours of trading. 

Offer Details

The offer price is expected to be at a 20-35% discount to the most recent estimated net asset value and dividend reinvestment price. That will be determined when tender offers start after a 10-business day notice period for each BDC, Cox and Saba said.

Both the firms sent the notice to Blue Owl Capital Corporation II on February 17, 2026 of their intention to commence a tender offer to purchase a portion of outstanding shares of OBDC II in cash.

The tender offers would provide a liquidity solution to retail investors in the wake of a significant industry-wide increase in BDC redemption requests, multiple quarters of net outflows and a rise in redemption gate provisions.

Blue Owl’s Redemption Halt

Blue Owl said on Thursday that it will permanently stop allowing redemptions from Blue Owl Capital Corp. II, a semi-liquid private credit fund marketed to U.S. retail investors.

The announcement showed Blue Owl abandoning an earlier plan to reopen withdrawals later this quarter. Instead, the firm said it would begin returning investor capital.

Blue Owl said it was “not halting redemptions” but instead “changing the method” by which investors receive their cash. The firm said it remains well capitalized, with about $4 billion in total cash and borrowing capacity, and described its balance sheet as “well positioned” to support portfolio performance into 2026.

How Did Stocktwits’ Users React?

Retail sentiment around OWL trended in ‘extremely bullish’ territory amid ‘extremely high’ message volume. 

Shares in the company have fallen 54.5% over the past year.

data.title: Cloudastructure Received Notice of Noncompliance
data.description:
On February 17, 2026, the company received notice from Nasdaq that it is not in compliance with Listing Rule 5550(a)(2) due to failing to maintain a minimum $1.00 bid price for 30 consecutive business days. The company has 180 days, until August 17, 2026, to regain compliance by achieving a $1.00 closing bid for at least 10 consecutive business days; an additional 180-day extension may be available, and options such as a reverse stock split may be considered. The notice has no immediate effect on the listing.

Original SEC Filing: CLOUDASTRUCTURE, INC. [ CSAI ] - 8-K - Feb. 20, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Cruise operator Carnival to unify dual listing
data.description:
Carnival will unify its parallel listings in New York and London and redomicile to Bermuda, the cruise operator said on Friday.

The UK‑listed Carnival Plc is expected to become a wholly owned subsidiary and the combined parent will continue to trade on the New York Stock Exchange.

The company, which is currently domiciled in the Republic of Panama, will operate under the name "Carnival Corporation Ltd" after shifting to Bermuda following the unification, the company said in a filing.

Last month, Carnival forecast an upbeat annual profit above Wall Street estimates, boosted by resilient demand from affluent travelers.

Its stock is up about 30% over the last 12 months.

data.title: VERS: Quarterly revenue grew to $417,932, but net loss remains high amid ongoing funding and legal risks
data.description:
Revenue reached $417,932 for the quarter, with a net loss of $5.48 million, reflecting improved cost control and lower non-cash expenses. The company faces substantial going concern risks, ongoing litigation, and significant management changes, while continuing to invest heavily in R&D and commercialization.

Original document: VERSES AI Inc [VERS] SEC 10-Q Quarterly Report — Feb. 20 2026

data.title: Sands China Hosts The Second Macao Technological Innovation Exploration
data.description:
NORTHAMPTON, MA / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2026 / Published by Las Vegas Sands on January 15, 2026

As part of its ongoing commitment to supporting the growth of Macao entrepreneurs, Sands China hosted the second Macao Technological Innovation Exploration in Lisbon this past November. The robust five-day program enabled 38 delegates from 25 Macao enterprises and startups to explore innovation in technology, while fostering industry exchange between Macao and Portugal.

Jointly organized by Sands China and the Macao Economic and Technological Development Bureau and supported by the Macao Science and Technology Development Fund, the program is rooted in in the historical tie between Portugal and Macao, which was a Portuguese trading port for more than 400 years.

Sands China coordinated the visit through its Sands Resorts Incubation Centre, launched in 2023 to support technology entrepreneurs in building their capacity and generating business opportunities. The center helps entrepreneurs identify promising technology products and services, and then develop and refine them at Sands China properties.

The exploration began with delegate attendance at the Web Summit, the world's largest technology conference drawing more than 71,000 visitors from 157 countries. Entrepreneurs attended keynote speeches, master class sharing sessions, and forums on artificial intelligence and robotics, in which they learned about cutting-edge technologies and connected with global technology leaders.

In addition, a Technology Business Networking Forum linked entrepreneurs to create dialogue within the group and with Lisbon technology enterprises. The networking event featured a panel discussion with four Portuguese enterprises and five Macao technology companies. Three of the Macao companies and two from Portugal gave presentations on their business models to foster opportunities for collaboration.

The delegation also visited Portuguese enterprises, where they gained in-depth understanding of innovative models, explored practical applications and established connections. Finally, Sands China hosted a Concrete Opportunities Workshop, which engaged delegates in collaborative discussions on opportunities for innovation.

The workshop featured Manuel Tanger, a faculty member in Disruption, Innovation and Entrepreneurship at Singularity University Portugal. Tanger provided an overview of technology innovation in Lisbon and insights for working with startups. He also guided participants in a collaborative exploration to map out innovation opportunities and determine next steps.

"Last year's inaugural trip to Lisbon was widely acclaimed," Grant Chum, chief executive officer and executive director of Sands China, said. "This year, the five-day trip returned with even greater impact, providing a forward-looking international platform for a new cohort of Macao enterprises to broaden their horizons. It injected fresh momentum into innovation while establishing meaningful connections with Lisbon companies and achieving tangible outcomes."

The Macao Technological Innovation Exploration in Lisbon is aligned with Sands China's support for the Macao SAR government's promotion of long-term and sustainable development of smart tourism and high-tech industries. Sands China aims to further integrate tourism and modern technology by supporting local research and development of new high-tech products that foster Macao's development as a world center of tourism and leisure.

The Lisbon exploration program also aligns with Sands China's extensive initiatives to promote small and medium enterprises in support of the health and prosperity of Macao and the Greater Bay Area region. To learn more about Sands' support for local businesses in its regions around the world, read the company's latest ESG report: https://www.sands.com/resources/reports/.

Find more stories and multimedia from Las Vegas Sands at 3blmedia.com.

Contact Info:

Spokesperson: Las Vegas Sands

Website: https://www.3blmedia.com/profiles/las-vegas-sands

Email: info@3blmedia.com

SOURCE: Las Vegas Sands

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai Sells Over $9.8 Million in Company Stock
data.description:

The sales were conducted at weighted average prices ranging from $302.07 to $306.09 across four transactions. Following these transactions, Pichai directly owns 2,818,524 shares of Class C Capital Stock and 227,560 shares of Class A Common Stock of Alphabet. data.title: Alaska Air Group EVP and CFO Shane R Tackett Sells 24,000 Shares
data.description:
Shane R Tackett, EVP and CFO of Alaska Air Group, sold 24,000 shares of common stock on February 18, 2026, at a weighted average price of $57.1472, totaling $1,371,532. Following the transaction, Tackett directly owns 43,377 shares and indirectly owns 2,806 shares through an ESOP Trust.

Reporter NameTackett Shane R
RelationshipEVP AND CFO
TypeSell
Amount$1,371,532
SEC FilingForm 4

SEC Filing: ALASKA AIR GROUP, INC. [ ALK ] - Form 4 - Feb. 20, 2026

data.title: Mediaalpha Inc expected to post earnings of 23 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Mediaalpha Inc , is expected to show a fall in quarterly revenue when it reports results on February 23 for the period ending December 31 2025

  • The Los Angeles California-based company is expected to report a 1.0% decrease in revenue to $297.72 million from $300.65 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 7 analysts, based on LSEG data.The company's guidance on October 29 2025, for the period ended December 31, was for revenue between $280.00 million and $300.00 million.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Mediaalpha Inc is for earnings of 23 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 6 "strong buy" or "buy," 2 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Mediaalpha Inc is $15.00, about 91.3% above its last closing price of $7.84

The company's guidance on October 29 2025 for the period ended December 31 was for Earnings before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation and Amortization of between USD280 million and USD29.5 million.

data.title: Regional Management presents investor presentation highlighting $2.1B receivables, 13.1% YoY portfolio growth
data.description:
Regional Management shared an investor presentation highlighting a $2.1 billion receivables portfolio and 13.1% year-over-year portfolio growth.

Key Highlights:

  • Management posted a presentation (available on regionalmanagement.com) for investor and banker meetings starting Feb 23, 2026.
  • Company reports $2.1B total receivables across 353 branches in 19 states as of 12/31/2025.
  • Achieved 13.1% YoY portfolio growth driven by new branches, auto-secured and higher-margin small loans.
  • Auto-secured loans grew $87.7M to 13.7% of portfolio; loans >36% APR represent 17.9% of portfolio.
  • Strong credit coverage: 4Q25 allowance for credit losses $174.4M, 145% of 30+ delinquency ($119.9M).

Original SEC Filing: Regional Management Corp. [ RM ] - 8-K - Feb. 20, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: RCI Hospitality Holdings Disclosed Failure to Satisfy a Continued Listing Rule or Standard
data.description:
On February 18, 2026, the company received notice from Nasdaq that it is not in compliance with Listing Rule 5250(c)(1) due to not filing its FY2025 Form 10-K and Q1 FY2026 Form 10-Q. The company has until March 31, 2026 to submit a compliance plan, with a potential exception period through June 29, 2026; the notification has no immediate effect on its Nasdaq listing.

Original SEC Filing: RCI HOSPITALITY HOLDINGS, INC. [ RICK ] - 8-K - Feb. 20, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Katapult Secures Waiver of Loan Covenant Default From Midtown Madison Management
data.description:
Katapult entered into an Eighth Limited Waiver to its Amended and Restated Loan and Security Agreement on Feb 13, 2026. The waiver, provided by Midtown Madison Management and other lenders, permanently clears a default triggered by not meeting the minimum trailing three-month net originations as of Jan 31, 2026. The action is intended to preserve access to the company's financing facility and avoid default remedies while operations continue.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Limited Waiver to Amended and Restated Loan and Security Agreement
  • Counterparty: Midtown Madison Management and other lenders
  • Signed / Effective: Feb 13 2026 / same
  • Reason: Address covenant breach and preserve liquidity

Original SEC Filing: Katapult Holdings, Inc. [ KPLT ] - 8-K - Feb. 20, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Jupiter Neurosciences Amends $6 Million Yorkville Convertible Notes, Installments Start Apr 1
data.description:
Jupiter Neurosciences amended its convertible promissory notes issued under the Standby Equity Purchase Agreement with Yorkville. The Omnibus Amendment delays the start of monthly installment payments to April 1, 2026, adopts a variable payment schedule, and updates provisions for Advance Notices. The company may satisfy installments in cash, through Advance Repayment, or a combination.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Amendment to convertible promissory notes and SEPA provisions
  • Counterparty: Yorkville
  • Signed / Effective: Feb 20 2026 / same
  • Reason: Extend and revise installment payment terms

Original SEC Filing: JUPITER NEUROSCIENCES, INC. [ JUNS ] - 8-K - Feb. 20, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: US judge questions DOJ handling of Washington Post reporter search
data.description:
Key points:
  • Judge questions DOJ's omission of 1980 privacy law
  • Prosecutors say law did not apply in national security probe
  • Washington Post seeking return of seized material

By Andrew Goudsward

A U.S. judge on Friday sharply questioned Justice Department lawyers about their handling of a search of a Washington Post reporter's home last month.

U.S. Magistrate Judge William Porter pressed government lawyers to explain why they did not mention a 1980 law restricting searches of journalists when they sought approval to search the home of reporter Hannah Natanson as part of a national security investigation.

"How could you miss it? How could you say it doesn't apply?" Porter asked during a hearing in federal court in Alexandria, Virginia.

Porter, who ultimately approved the search, revealed that he had rejected prior versions of the proposed warrant.

DOJ LAWYERS' CONDUCT IN QUESTION

Porter became the latest judge in a high-profile case to question the conduct of DOJ lawyers under President Donald Trump's administration. Others have accused the Justice Department of making legal errors and defying court orders, claims the department has denied.

DOJ attorney Gordon Kromberg told Porter that prosecutors determined the 1980 law, known as the Privacy Protection Act, did not apply in situations in which the reporter was a participant in potential criminal conduct.

The FBI searched Natanson's Virginia home and seized cellphones and laptops last month as part of an investigation into the alleged leak of classified information to Natanson by a government contractor. The Washington Post has said the paper and Natanson are not targets of the probe.

A press freedom group has filed an ethics complaint against Kromberg, accusing him of withholding information from the judge. Porter told Kromberg he may be correct that the law did not prohibit the search, but vented frustration that prosecutors had not referenced it as a factor to consider.

Friday's hearing centered on the Post and Natanson's legal challenge to the search and demand that the government return the seized materials. Their lawyers described the search of a reporter's home based purely on newsgathering activity as unprecedented in the U.S.

Porter has temporarily blocked the government from searching the material while the challenge plays out.

Simon Latcovich, an attorney for the Post, said the raid "froze Ms. Natanson's newsgathering" and risked exposing more than 1,000 confidential government sources.

Justice Department lawyers argued the search was a necessary part of an investigation into the unlawful disclosure of U.S. government secrets. They said the DOJ planned to have a group of FBI agents not involved in the investigation, known as a filter team, review the seized material and separate anything not relevant to the probe.
